decision_summary: The chip temperature (174.6°C) severely exceeds the limit (45°C). The most effective immediate lever is to maximize the heat spreader's conductivity to its upper bound, as it is currently at 500 W/(m*K) which is the defined maximum. Since material tuning is capped, we must simultaneously increase the spreader's geometric cross-section (width and height) to enhance lateral spreading and vertical conduction paths.

changes.reason: Increase spreader width by 50% (max allowed step) to expand the lateral area for heat dissipation towards the cooled boundaries.
Increase spreader height by 50% (max allowed step) to improve vertical thermal conductance and reduce resistance between chip and spreader.

expected_effects: Significant reduction in chip peak temperature due to lower thermal resistance.
More uniform temperature distribution across the top layer.
Move feasibility status closer to satisfying the 45°C constraint.
risk_notes: Aggressive geometry changes may approach domain boundaries or create tight meshing requirements.
If this step is insufficient, further iterations will require adjusting base material conductivity or exploring heat source reduction scenarios.
Spreader width increase (1.05m) approaches the design domain width (1.2m), leaving small margins on sides.
